title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot continues his investigation into the unusual thefts and property damage at the student dormitory. While reviewing the list of missing items, he notes several oddities, such as Mr. McNab's old flannel pants, light bulbs, borax, bath salt, and various recipes. However, he identifies more suspicious items that suggest a different motive: a torn canvas backpack, which likely belongs to either Leonard Betson or Colin McNaughton, and a delicate green silk scarf belonging to Valerie Hobhouse that has been cut up. He also notes the disappearance of a single evening shoe belonging to Sally Finch, an American Fulbright scholar. Poirot suspects that the destruction of the scarf and the backpack was motivated by malice rather than simple theft.

To investigate the missing shoe, Poirot instructs Miss Lemon to have Mrs. Haberdasher report the loss to the lost property office at Baker Street Station or the Lambeth Police Station, suggesting she claim it was lost in a taxi or on a train. Additionally, Poirot pressures Mrs. Haberdasher to provide information regarding the students' personal relationships, temperaments, and potential jealousies, suspecting she may be protecting someone she likes.

Later, Mrs. Haberdasher returns to 26 Mountnut Street, where she is greeted by Leonard "Lennie" Bateson. Lennie makes gruesome jokes about "chopping up a lovely corpse" and mentions a reaction from a girl named Sylvia. He is joined by Nigel Chapman, a lanky, long-haired young man. Before Mrs. Haberdasher can settle in, she is informed that Mrs. Nicolaitis wishes to see her.
